_meta.type_rationale: Financial services — DNI and P/B prioritised
_meta.method_explanation: Consumer finance companies — such as credit card providers or personal lenders — earn income from the interest they charge on loans. We use net income DCF and Price-to-Book, which together capture both the earning power and the asset value of the loan book.

_notes.shares_vs_buybacks: When a company buys back its own shares, it reduces the total number of shares in circulation — which means each remaining share represents a larger slice of the business. _notes.returns_trajectory: Return on Equity (ROE) measures how much profit a company generates for every dollar of shareholder money invested. Return on Invested Capital (ROIC) is similar but also includes debt. Both are expressed as a percentage — higher is better. A company consistently earning above 15% ROIC is generally considered to have a genuine competitive advantage over its peers.
_notes.revenue_vs_ar: Accounts receivable is money the company is owed by customers who have not yet paid their bills. This chart compares revenue growth to accounts receivable growth each year. If accounts receivable is growing much faster than revenue, it may mean the company is struggling to collect payments — sometimes a sign of aggressive or fictitious revenue reporting. A healthy company should see both lines move at a similar pace.

company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: Daktronics, Inc. designs, manufactures, and sells electronic scoreboards, programmable display systems, and large screen video displays for sporting, commercial, and transportation applications in the United States and internationally. It operates through Commercial, Live Events, High School Park and Recreation, Transportation, and International segments. The company offers video display and walls; scoreboards and timing systems; LED message displays and sings; intelligent transportation systems dynamic message signs; mass transit display; sound systems; and digital billboards and street furniture, and digit and price displays. It also provides indoor dynamic messaging systems; and software and controllers, which includes Venus, a control suite software to control the creation of messages and graphic sequences for uploading to displays. The company serves out-of-home companies, retailers, quick-serve restaurants, casinos, shopping centers, cruise ships, commercial building owners, petroleum retailers, governmental transportation departments, transportation industry contractors, airlines, and sports and commercial business facilities. The company distinguishes itself through integrated software solutions like Venus, its control suite enabling message creation and graphic sequencing, alongside specialized systems for mass transit, intelligent transportation, and out-of-home advertising markets.

company_profile.description: Daré Bioscience, Inc. develops therapies in the areas of contraception, pelvic pain, fertility, infectious disease, menopause, and sexual and vaginal health in the United States. The company offers XACIATO, a lincosamide antibacterial gel for the treatment of bacterial vaginosis. It also develops Ovaprene, a hormone-free monthly intravaginal contraceptive; Sildenafil Cream, a cream formulation of sildenafil for topical administration to the female genitalia for the treatment of female sexual arousal disorder; DARE-HRT1 to treat vasomotor symptoms in menopausal hormone therapy; DARE-VVA1 for the treatment of dyspareunia or pain during sexual intercourse; and DARE-HPV, a fixed-dose formulation of lopinavir and ritonavir in a soft gel vaginal insert to treat genital human papillomavirus (HPV) infection, cervical intraepithelial neoplasia, and other HPV-related pathologies. In addition, the company develops DARE-PDM1, a hydrogel formulation of diclofenac for vaginal administration to treat primary dysmenorrhea; DARE-204 and DARE-214, injectable formulations of etonogestrel that provides contraception over 6-month and 12-month periods; DARE-FRT1 and DARE-PTB1, an intravaginal ring designed to deliver bio-identical progesterone for luteal phase support as part of an in vitro fertilization treatment plan; and DARE-PTB2 for the prevention of preterm birth. Further, it develops DARE-LARC1, a contraceptive implant delivering levonorgestrel with a woman-centered design controlled contraceptive option; DARE-RH1, an approach to non-hormonal contraception for men and women by targeting the CatSper ion channel; DARE-PTB2 for the prevention and treatment of idiopathic preterm birth through inhibition of a stress response protein; and DARE-GML, which delivers antimicrobial glycerol monolaurate intravaginally. Daré Bioscience, Inc. was founded in 2015 and is headquartered in San Diego, California.
company_profile.description_summary:
This bioscience company develops therapies for women's reproductive and sexual health, including contraception, fertility, menopause, and infectious disease treatments. Its primary revenue source is XACIATO, a gel for treating bacterial vaginosis. The company also commercializes various contraceptive and hormone therapies in development.

The company distinguishes itself through a woman-centered approach to reproductive health, offering innovative delivery methods including vaginal gels, intravaginal rings, and implants targeting underserved conditions like dyspareunia and primary dysmenorrhea.

bull_points: DBCA completed its IPO in February 2026 and holds approximately $288 million in trust with an additional $864,356 in working capital, providing substantial dry powder for an imminent business combination announcement within the next 6 months.

The company's management team, led by David Boral and John Darwin, has extensive SPAC and M&A transaction expertise, increasing the probability of completing a strategically sound business combination that could unlock shareholder value quickly.

The recent separation of Class A shares and warrants (effective February 25, 2026) increased trading liquidity and reduced unit-lock constraints, potentially attracting new institutional investors ahead of a business combination announcement.
bear_points: DBCA was unable to timely file its Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2025, signaling potential internal compliance or operational delays that could extend timelines for announcing a business combination target.

As a newly formed blank-check company with $288 million in trust but zero revenue-generating operations and a reported net loss of $66,845 in its first operating period, DBCA has no operating leverage to drive near-term returns.

The company has only 18 months (extendable) to complete a business combination before shareholders can redeem public shares, creating deal deadline pressure that could force acceptance of unfavorable transaction terms.

bull_points: The D. Boral Capital ecosystem is actively sourcing emerging-technology acquisition targets, as evidenced by D. Boral ARC Acquisition I's combination with Exascale—demonstrating a proven sourcing pipeline for high-growth, capital-efficient companies in hot sectors like AI infrastructure.

The sponsor's track record spans traditional IPOs, SPAC acquisitions, and restructurings, suggesting DBCA's eventual target will likely operate in a market with structural tailwinds and benefit from experienced operational governance.

DBCA's blank-check structure allows substantial flexibility in deal design, financing, and go-public valuation—enabling the company to capture asymmetric upside if the ultimate target operates in a high-growth category (similar to AI infrastructure, software, or emerging tech) during a strong capital-markets environment.
bear_points: DBCA's current financial metrics show zero profitability, zero growth, zero ROIC, and zero ROE across all quality scores, with a current ratio of just 0.22—post-merger performance depends entirely on the undisclosed target's execution, creating significant integration and operational execution risk.

SPAC mergers historically underperform relative to traditional IPOs post-combination, especially when sponsors maintain significant founder equity and control; DBCA's sponsor and management control approximately 63% of shares combined, creating potential misalignment with public shareholders.

The company has generated no revenue and posted net losses; any target combination that does not immediately generate strong cash flow and demonstrate clear unit economics will likely trade below $10.00 and face shareholder redemption pressure, destroying long-term value.

bull_points: Q4 2025 marked accelerating momentum with 32% Y/Y order growth and 39% Y/Y Marketplace GOV growth to $29.7B, demonstrating strong demand resilience despite macro uncertainties heading into Q1 2026.

Deliveroo acquisition contributed over $45M of Adjusted EBITDA in Q4 2025 alone, with management projecting ~$200M full-year contribution in 2026, providing a near-term earnings catalyst for 2H 2026 as integration synergies expand.

Stock trades at 23.6% margin of safety below intrinsic value of $209.68, offering a technical entry point especially given strong 38% full-year revenue growth and 51% Y/Y GAAP net income growth in Q4 2025.
bear_points: Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.48 missed consensus by $0.11 (-18.6% surprise) despite revenue beat, signaling margin compression from aggressive tech and Deliveroo integration spending that management warned will depress Q1 2026 Adjusted EBITDA guidance to $675-775M, below Q4's $780M.

Q1 2026 guidance incorporates estimated $20M headwind from severe weather impacts and rising Dasher costs per order, both structural pressures that could persist through 2H 2026 if macro conditions deteriorate.

Recent analyst price target resets—moving from ~$231 to $212—reflect skepticism about margin expansion despite revenue growth, suggesting the market is pricing in lower profitability assumptions than reflected in current valuation estimates.

bull_points: DoorDash demonstrates solid financial quality with 33.1% revenue growth, 6.3% profit margin, and improving profitability (GAAP net income grew 51% Y/Y to $935M in 2025) while maintaining a healthy 1.41 current ratio and moderate 2.43x debt-to-EBITDA, positioning it to invest in growth without balance sheet stress.

With a projected 5-year EPS CAGR of 48.8%, the company is on pace to generate $100B+ in combined merchant sales and Dasher earnings in 2026, reflecting multiple expansion avenues: Deliveroo synergies, grocery/retail penetration, international scale, and autonomous delivery commercialization planned for 2026.

AI-powered advertising platform expansion with $175M Symbiosys acquisition targets $1B+ run-rate ad revenue, a high-margin diversification lever that mirrors the success seen in Uber's ad business and mitigates reliance on core delivery economics.
bear_points: Despite 33.1% revenue growth, profitability metrics remain constrained: 6.3% profit margin, 9.92% ROE, and 7.63% ROIC are modest relative to growth expectations, and management's stated strategy of reinvesting aggressively in tech platform and Deliveroo integration suggests margin expansion may not materialize near-term, limiting EPS upside.

Rising Dasher labor costs per order and regulatory headwinds in core markets (mentioned in Q1 2026 guidance and management commentary) create a structural profit ceiling, while 2.43x debt-to-EBITDA provides limited financial flexibility if macro consumer spending deteriorates as management cautioned.

Competitive intensity remains fierce with Uber, Amazon, and regional players, and the company's 5-year growth quality score is only 5/10 with constrained predictability (score of 6/10), suggesting long-term growth acceleration to justify 23.6% upside to intrinsic value depends on flawless autonomous delivery and international expansion execution.

watchlist_signal.reason: Good fundamentals with a 24% margin of safety — worth adding at current prices.

The company's cloud platform consists of a learning suite, which includes Docebo Learn platform, a cloud-based learning platform that allows learning administrators to deliver personalized learning; Docebo Content Marketplace, an access to off-the-shelf learning content and provide predeveloped learning content; Insights module allows organizations to understand the results of learning programs with data visualizations; Learning Evaluation module to incorporate the learner's perspective into analyses by collection of feedback; and Advanced Analytics Pack to integrate learning data into data ecosystem and BI tool. It also offers Communities module enabling interactive learner communities; eCommerce module that monetize from digital training contents, as well as manage and sells training offerings; eCommerce module to monetize training programs; Docebo Integrations; Headless Learning allows businesses to build learning experiences outside of the Docebo learning environment; Harmony Search, an AI-powered search capability. In addition, the company provides Docebo Creator enables organizations to design, scale, and deploy learning contents; Docebo for Salesforce, an integration of Salesforce's APIs and technology architecture to deliver a learning experience within Salesforce workflows; Docebo Embed (OEM) enables original equipment manufacturers to embed and resell the Docebo learning platform; Docebo Branded Mobile App Publisher, allows organizations to create and distribute a branded version of Docebo's mobile learning application; Docebo Extended Enterprise supports customer education, partner enablement, and retention by enabling organizations to train external audiences from a single LMS; and Docebo for Microsoft Teams. The company was founded in 2005 and is headquartered in Toronto, Canada.
company_profile.description_summary:
Docebo develops a cloud-based learning management platform that enables organizations to deliver personalized training content. The platform includes modules for content delivery, analytics, learner feedback, and community engagement, with additional tools for content creation and ecommerce functionality. Revenue primarily derives from subscription fees for platform access.

The company differentiates through specialized offerings including AI-powered search, headless learning capabilities for custom experiences, and purpose-built solutions for extended enterprise training, partner enablement, and integration with popular business applications like Salesforce and Microsoft Teams.

_meta.type_rationale: FCF compounder — FCF margin 17.2%; DCF methods prioritised
_meta.method_explanation: Free cash flow compounders are businesses that consistently generate more cash than they need to run the business — think of companies like consumer goods brands or software firms with subscription revenue. Because their cash flows are reliable year after year, a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model works very well. DCF asks: if I add up all the future cash flows this company will generate and adjust for the time value of money, what is it worth today? For these companies, that calculation gives a meaningful and trustworthy answer.

company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: Decoy Therapeutics Inc. operates as a pre-clinical stage biotechnology company. It focuses on advancing the peptide conjugate therapeutics through its Immediate Peptide/PPMO/P-PROTAC Alpha-helical Conjugate Technology (IMP3ACT) platform that leverages peptide coiled-coils chemistry and physics to design a-helical peptides through computational and ML tools. The company's IMP3ACT platform leverages machine learning and artificial intelligence tools alongside synthesis techniques to engineer, enhance, and manufacture peptide conjugates targeting various unmet medical needs. It focuses on building a portfolio of peptide conjugate therapeutics focusing on infectious diseases and oncology. The company was formerly known as Salarius Pharmaceuticals, Inc. and change its name to Decoy Therapeutics Inc. in January 2026. Decoy Therapeutics Inc. is based in Houston, Texas.
company_profile.description_summary:
This pre-clinical stage biotechnology company develops peptide conjugate therapeutics targeting infectious diseases and oncology. It leverages its IMP3ACT platform, which uses machine learning and peptide coiled-coils chemistry to design alpha-helical peptides for various unmet medical needs. The company generates no current revenue as it remains in pre-clinical development.

The IMP3ACT platform distinguishes Decoy through its integration of computational tools and artificial intelligence with synthesis techniques to engineer enhanced peptide conjugates, differentiating its approach in the therapeutic peptide space.

company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: Delcath Systems, Inc., an interventional oncology company, focuses on the treatment of primary and metastatic liver cancers in the United States and Europe. The company's lead product candidate is HEPZATO KIT, a melphalan for injection/hepatic delivery system to administer high-dose chemotherapy to the liver while controlling systemic exposure and associated side effects. Its clinical development program for HEPZATO is the FOCUS clinical trial for patients with metastatic hepatic dominant Uveal Melanoma to investigate objective response rate in metastatic uveal melanoma. The company also provides HEPZATO as a stand-alone medical device under the CHEMOSAT Hepatic Delivery System trade name for Melphalan or CHEMOSAT for medical centers to treat a range of liver cancers in Europe. The company was founded in 1988 and is headquartered in Queensbury, New York.
company_profile.description_summary: This interventional oncology company develops treatments for primary and metastatic liver cancers across the US and Europe. Its lead product, HEPZATO KIT, delivers high-dose chemotherapy directly to the liver while minimizing systemic exposure and side effects. The company also markets CHEMOSAT, a hepatic delivery system for melphalan, to treat various liver cancers in European medical centers.

company_profile.name: Liberty Defense Holdings, Ltd.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: Liberty Defense Holdings, Ltd. engages in the development and commercialization of advanced security detection technologies. The company offers AI-based contactless security technology for detecting concealed metallic and non-metallic weapons and threats. It provides two separate security screening solutions, the HEXWAVE system and the HD-AIT Upgrade Kit. The company's flagship product, HEXWAVE, is a walk-through security portal that utilizes millimeter wave technology and 3D imaging to detect concealed metallic and non-metallic weapons, including traditional metallic guns and knives as well as more novel threats such as liquid, plastic, and powder explosives, 3D printed guns, and other prohibited items. In addition, its High-Definition Advanced Imaging Technology (HD-AIT) is used for body and shoe scanning. The company sells its products to regional airports, courthouses and correctional facilities, universities, and research laboratories. The company was formerly known as Gulfstream Acquisition 1 Corp. and changed its name to Liberty Defense Holdings, Ltd. in April 2019. Liberty Defense Holdings, Ltd. was incorporated in 2012 and is headquartered in Wilmington, Massachusetts.
company_profile.description_summary:
This company develops and commercializes AI-powered security detection technology designed to identify concealed weapons and threats. Its primary revenue source comes from selling the HEXWAVE walk-through security portal and HD-AIT scanning systems to airports, courthouses, prisons, universities, and research facilities.

What distinguishes the company is its millimeter wave and 3D imaging technology capable of detecting both traditional metallic weapons and novel threats like 3D-printed guns, explosives, and prohibited powders—offering contactless screening across diverse security settings.

The company differentiates itself through proprietary IntelLigence communication technology, enabling real-time battery monitoring, instant alerts, and advanced protection across diverse applications from RVs to off-grid residential systems.

company_profile.name: DeFi Development Corp.
company_profile.type_rationale: Asset manager/broker — DNI prioritised; standard CF metrics unreliable
company_profile.description: DeFi Development Corp. focuses on building its treasury around Solana (SOL). SOL is a blockchain infrastructure platform. The company operates through two segments: Digital Asset Treasury and Real Estate Platform. The Digital Asset Treasury segment executes and manages its treasury policy for the purpose of purchasing, holding, and compounding its digital asset holdings through staking and operating its owned validators. The Real Estate Platform segment offers a technology platform that connects commercial mortgage and small business borrowers looking for debt to refinance, build, or buy commercial property, including apartment buildings and commercial property lenders. The company was formerly known as Janover Inc. and changed its name to DeFi Development Corp. in April 2025. The company. was founded in 2018 and is headquartered in Boca Raton, Florida.
company_profile.description_summary:
This company builds a treasury centered on Solana blockchain assets while operating a real estate technology platform. Revenue derives from its Digital Asset Treasury segment, which purchases and compounds SOL holdings through staking and validator operations, alongside a Real Estate Platform connecting commercial mortgage borrowers and lenders for property refinancing and acquisition.

The company distinguishes itself by combining cryptocurrency treasury management with a commercial real estate lending marketplace, bridging digital assets and traditional real estate finance.

_meta.type_rationale: Asset manager/broker — DNI prioritised; standard CF metrics unreliable
_meta.method_explanation: Asset managers and brokers earn fees for managing other people's money. Their balance sheets hold client assets that do not belong to the firm, which makes standard balance sheet metrics like Price-to-Book misleading. Instead, we focus on net income and revenue-based methods, which better reflect the underlying fee-earning power of the business.

quality.company_type_note: Banks and REITs carry structural leverage — do not describe financial_strength scores of 7+ as 'moderate concern' for these types.
quality.note:
Moat score is null — AI Studio adds moat (0-10) and recalculates.
With moat: final_pct = (profitability + financial_strength + growth + predictability + moat) / 50 × 100.
Then re-classify using tiebreaker_inputs:
  ≥70%      → Safe (no penalty)
  65–69%    → Safe if 2+ of (roe≥0.13, roic≥0.13, eps_next_5y≥0.10) else Speculative (−3pts)
  55–64%    → Speculative (−3pts)
  50–54%    → Speculative if 2+ of (roe≥0.08, roic≥0.08, eps_next_5y≥0.08) else Dangerous (−8pts)
  <50%      → Dangerous (−8pts)
Moat override: if moat≥7 AND (profitability≥7 OR fs≥7) AND pct≥55 → Safe.
final_score_pct = max(0, final_pct − penalty)

_notes.revenue_vs_net_income: This chart shows how much money the company brings in (revenue) versus how much it actually keeps as profit (net income) each year. A healthy company should show both lines growing over time. If revenue grows but net income shrinks, the company is spending more than it is earning — a warning sign worth investigating.
_notes.fcf_vs_debt: Free cash flow is the cash left over after the company has paid for everything it needs to run and grow the business. This chart compares that cash to the company's total debt. A company with rising free cash flow and falling debt is in a strong financial position. If debt is rising faster than cash, the company may be taking on more risk than it can comfortably manage.
_notes.shares_vs_buybacks: When a company buys back its own shares, it reduces the total number of shares in circulation — which means each remaining share represents a larger slice of the business. This chart shows how the share count has changed over time. Consistent buybacks are generally a sign that management believes the stock is undervalued and wants to return cash to shareholders.
_notes.returns_trajectory: Return on Equity (ROE) measures how much profit a company generates for every dollar of shareholder money invested. Return on Invested Capital (ROIC) is similar but also includes debt. Both are expressed as a percentage — higher is better. A company consistently earning above 15% ROIC is generally considered to have a genuine competitive advantage over its peers.
_notes.revenue_vs_ar: Accounts receivable is money the company is owed by customers who have not yet paid their bills. This chart compares revenue growth to accounts receivable growth each year. If accounts receivable is growing much faster than revenue, it may mean the company is struggling to collect payments — sometimes a sign of aggressive or fictitious revenue reporting. A healthy company should see both lines move at a similar pace.
